slide_1_Cover: End-to-End Infrastructure for Globalizing East Asian IP
slide_2_Idea_Problem_Solution_Value: Unlock global revenue from underutilized East Asian IP, with proven Western demand (37K users, $310K revenue, $0 CAC)
Reduce localization cost by 96% while preserving cultural fidelity — enabling faster, scalable global releases
Capture and protect revenue with an end-to-end platform spanning localization, distribution, and piracy enforcement
Genesis Studio — a global reading platform with readers from 193 countries and 80M+ page views, creating a direct revenue channel for publishers at $0 CAC
Babel AI — a purpose-built localization engine that retains the author's voice and cultural nuance, reducing translation cost from $500 to $20/episode and years to days
Nazgul — an automated enforcement agent that detects pirated content across the web and issues takedowns automatically with zero manpower required
Only 0.5% of East Asian IP has been globalized; most titles earn zero overseas revenue
Human translation costs $500 per episode and take years, while generic AI loses the author's voice, style, and cultural nuance
Machine Translation Post-Editing (MTPE) has slashed the cost and time of localization. Certainly no logner at $500, but not at $20 per episode
Delays fuel piracy, diverting readers and revenue from IP owners
slide_3_Market_1_3_TAM_SAM_SOM_+_Why_Now: Saturated domestically, Korean publishers sit on massive unmonetized IP backlogs, desperate for affordable localization to unlock global revenue
Explosive Western demand (validated by Netflix's $2.5B IP commitment) is colliding with breakthrough AI localization, solving the industry's unit economic bottleneck
WEBTOON, Tapas, Tappytoon prove East Asian IP travels — 193 countries already on Genesis platform at $0 CAC
Asian Webtoon + Web novel IP consumed in the West
Webtoon is growing at CAGR 33.10%, projected to be $60.25B by 2031
Web novel is growing at CAGR 3.8%, projected to be $14B by 2031
Western webtoon and web novel market is ~$7B(30% of the global), and 60 to 70% of the content originates from Korea and China
Webtoon users are between 200 and 400M, and web novel between 500 and 800M. With the overlap, 700M to 1B global readers
If Genesis Studio captures 1% of global users, and convert 3 to 5%, it can be 200 to 500K paying users
slide_4_Market_2_3_Competition: Global scale + data flywheel with $1.6B market cap; audience and distribution already locked in
Global-first Korean IP operator with 8M+ readers; closest execution benchmark for Genesis model
AI-based IP protection with $90M raised at ~$313M valuation; narrow wedge, clearer enterprise ROI
E2E localization service acquired by Kakao Piccoma; tool-heavy without own distribution platform
Unlike generic LLM wrappers, Babel AI builds a proprietary translation memory database with every title processed, creating a compounding, defensible data moat for cultural nuance
Unlike traditional agency vendors, Genesis actually owns the end consumer (Zero-CAC distribution pipeline to an engaged audience across 193 countries)

Babel AI acts as a B2B wedge to acquire high-value IP, which is then directly monetized on their proprietary B2C platform and safeguarded by automated piracy enforcement—allowing them to own and protect the entire cross-border lifecycle
slide_5_Market_3_3_GTM_Pricing_BM_Scale: B2B: upfront localization fee, or 50/50 revenue share with publishers
Publisher localization fees + B2C subscription revenue
3 paying publishers and $310K lifetime revenue with $0 CAC, validating the revenue model pre-investment

Phase 1: everage current pilots (Qimao, Storeinlab, Inkfiles) to validate Babel AI’s speed and 96% cost savings. Convert these pilots into long-term 50/50 revenue-share contracts to build a massive, exclusive IP library at zero upfront content cost.

Phase 2: Utilize the acquired IP to activate the existing 37K user base. Instead of relying solely on a high-friction $29.99/mo subscription, optimize the funnel through a proven freemium/micro-transaction model (pay-per-chapter), transitioning organic traffic from 193 countries into recurring revenue.
Phase 3: Open Babel to Japanese Manga and Chinese studios. Establish Genesis as the default IP operating system across SEA, LatAm, and Europe.

Unlike legacy localization agencies that scale linearly with headcount, Genesis's automated pipeline (Babel + Distribution + Nazgul) processes new publisher catalogs with near-zero marginal cost, unlocking software-like gross margins for media distribution.

Every localized title compounds Babel’s translation memory database, creating a highly specialized linguistic engine trained on East Asian cultural nuances and slang. Because this core AI is format-agnostic, Genesis can seamlessly scale beyond webtoons to capture the broader Media & Entertainment TAM—effortlessly expanding into gaming localization, animation, and OTT/K-Drama subtitling.

CTO Daniel is an ex-Capital One/Global Relay/Circle Global engineer who built systems at scale in regulated environments and has been an avid webnovel reader for a decade → technical depth meets domain passion
CBO David brings extensive B2B enterprise sales experience critical for publisher partnerships and future enterprise expansion → the commercial engine for scaling beyond B2C

slide_7_Team_2_4_Program_Trajectory: Razeen joined Antler Korea with Genesis Studio, an existing platform with 37K users. Met Daniel and David on Day 1 and formed the team after the Shark Tank pitch event
Daniel and David initially paired together, but after discovering Razeen was the founder of Genesis Studio — the platform Daniel actively used — the three formed a team over dinner
CEO drives product and vision, CTO leads engineering and technical infrastructure, CBO handles B2B sales and business development; clear domain ownership from Day 1
Received Green signal in Bootcamp 1 with feedback:  ready to track out' — immediate recognition of strong traction and team-market fit
Tracked out in Bootcamp 2 with Gabriel as partner. Feedback emphasized demonstrating technical advantage with high confidence and submitting very specific execution plans
IC Sponsored by JaeHee. Group Office Hour feedback focused on opportunity sizing of publishers, MG economics, and pipeline conversion strategy
Post-trackout: executing publisher partnerships with Qimao, Storeinlab, and Inkfiles while scaling the B2C platform in the US/UK market
Summarizes the journey from program start through team formation and PMF validation, based on weekly founder retrospectives and program team observations
slide_8_Team_3_4_Evaluation: 3 co-founders, 72–90h/wk aligned. Bonded via domain passion. CTO active platform user
37K users, $310K rev, $0 CAC (solo founder). 3 publisher deals. BC1 Green—track-out ready
Fired founding member for insufficient commitment. All 3 express pivot-readiness if broken
Deep publisher pain expertise. Co-founded Novelupdates (Directory of Asian translated novels). TAM needs work
96% cost reduction via Babel AI (nuance preserved). Piracy detection automated.
End-to-end: localization+distribution+enforcement. Each title compounds memory DB—generic AI cannot
Based on weekly founder retrospectives and feedback
slide_9_Team_4_4_Strengths_&_Improvement: Strengths
● $310K revenue and 37K users built at $0 CAC
Organic traction across 193 countries with 80M+ page views proves both Western demand and distribution capability without any marketing spend
● 41.47% paid recurring rate validates willingness to pay
Exceptionally high conversion rate for a content platform demonstrates that properly localized East Asian IP commands premium pricing
● Babel AI delivers 96% cost reduction with cultural fidelity
Purpose-built localization engine reduces $500/episode to $20 while preserving author voice — a technical moat that compounds with each title
● 3 publisher partnerships already executing
Qimao (China), Storeinlab (Korea), and Inkfiles (US) represent cross-geography validation of the B2B model with both upfront and revenue-share structures
● Full-stack team with deep domain passion
All three founders are avid readers of East Asian content; CTO discovered the platform organically, ensuring authentic product intuition beyond commercial motivation
Areas for Improvement
● Paid marketing and social/UGC capability gap
Current growth is 100% organic; scaling to $1M+ revenue target requires dedicated marketing hires
● Minimum guarantee economics unresolved
Partner feedback flagged the MG problem — publisher advance structures could strain cash flow before revenue share materializes
● Visa dependency for CEO creates operational risk
CEO requires startup visa post-residency; immigration uncertainty could affect company continuity and fundraising timeline
Team (4/4)
Summary
Founder Alignment & Equity Structure
● Proposed equal split; co-founders suggested
slide_10_Investor_Assessment: Large TAM ($25B) growing at CAGR 30%, reaching $60B+ by 2031
The core AI technology is format-agnostic allowing for future expansion into the broader digital content (K-drama, game, and etc)
Well-funded incumbents like WEBTOON and Tappytoon that already dominate Western distribution
Genesis has a strong wedge by bypassing legacy human-localization costs, acting as a full-stack B2B2C pipeline
Babel AI builds a compounding  translation memory database with every title, creating a proprietary data moat for East Asian cultural nuance that generic LLMs cannot easily replicate
The automated localization pipeline processes new publisher catalogs with near-zero marginal cost
Reaching users across 193 countries with zero marketing spend (global demand is high)
Strong technical founder-problem fit, CEO’s AI/ML pedigree + CTO’s fintech-grade infrastructure capabilities

Only 0.5% of East Asian IP has been globalized. Genesis is not fighting for crumbs from WEBTOON; it is building a proprietary pipeline to monetize the tens of thousands of titles currently generating zero international revenue. Babel AI allows to process new publisher catalogs with near-zero marginal cost. Babel builds a proprietary translation memory database, compounding their edge in author voice and cultural nuance. However, the ability to acquire a large reader/user base and to convert free readers into recurring revenue remains unproven at scale.
